Art title 1: A Bible Falling Apart (by Cory Snyder at Dry Bones Studio)
Artist Statement 1:
"A bible falling apart usually belongs to someone who isn't" Charles Spurgeon
Now I'm not a book worm nor can I say I've dug into the word everyday in my walk nor am going to say you have to be reading the word every second of everyday. My belief and experience is the word of God is alive. Diving into the word breeds connection with Him, He reveals new things, old things, things He wants to speak to you about. I've realized over time a lot of followers don't like reading their bible bc it's forged from religion and "you must read your bible!" Yes we must bc it gives us life, resets our heart and aligns it with the Father but we do it because we get to, and what freedom we have to do so! The word comes alive with an expecting heart! The lords wants relationship with us. Next time you head into the word look at it as you are spending time with Jesus , because you are. (Cory Snyder with Dry Bones Studio)
excerpt:
In the wake of George Floyd's death, and that of Breonna Taylor, in the state where Ahmad Arbrey was shot, and in which too soon after police killed another black man, Rayshard Brooks, churches gathered.

13,000 black and white Christians came together in Atlanta to try again to exorcize racism from their city. Together they marched to the capitol, prayed for the new chief of police, worshipped with praise music that unified both races, and the preachers preached ways they and their churches were planning to rectify the racial inequalities and violence torturing their city.
 
Late in the afternoon one of the old black pastors said, "We did this very same thing back in the 90's but nothing changed. We asked for help but the white churches were silent." That looming apprehension was met by the hope that God could do it differently this time. We would have to try harder. 

There was a word that was preached into that crowd just before the masses were dismissed. It was in the middle of a bigger speech and passed through them like a wave, unnoticed by many. But it planted a seed in many who didn't even realize it. The word was "imagination." (see the stories in the next few days to see what happened)
3dNotes2: The ruins of ancient Babylon. If you look west on one of the hills you can see one of Saddam Hussein's abandoned palaces.
